<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] 29 files span config, CI, orchestration scripts, and guidelines — consider whether PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md and similar docs belong in the docs zone for cleaner separation.
[observation] [info] Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm the orchestration layer is correctly isolated — all cross-package coordination happens via spawned CLIs rather than library imports.
[observation] [info] Root-level orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) delegate to package CLIs rather than importing package APIs, which is the intended architectural pattern.
[relationship] [warning] The orchestration zone has no import edges to any other zone, meaning integration contract violations (e.g. changed CLI flags or output formats in rex/sourcevision/hench) will only manifest at runtime, not at build or typecheck time — consider adding a contract-test layer in e2e that validates CLI I/O contracts across package boundaries.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Root orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) are plain JS in a TypeScript monorepo, making cross-package API breakage undetectable at typecheck time. Even a full pnpm typecheck pass will not catch broken orchestration contracts.
[suggestion] [info] CODEX.md mirrors CLAUDE.md but with Codex-specific content — this multi-vendor instruction-file convention is established by practice but not by policy. A brief policy note in CONTRIBUTING.md describing the expected structure and naming for per-vendor AI instruction files would prevent inconsistently structured files as more vendors are supported.
[suggestion] [warning] config.js implements config-merging logic (deepMerge, loadJSON, saveJSON, validators) and directly reads/writes package config files rather than delegating to a spawned CLI, violating the orchestration tier's documented spawn-only rule. This is the only root orchestration script that breaks this invariant. Either extract a dedicated config subcommand in a domain package, or explicitly document config.js as a spawn-exempt exception in CLAUDE.md.
[suggestion] [info] web.js is a legacy alias with no deprecation marker in the file itself. Add a JSDoc @deprecated comment and a runtime console.warn to guide contributors toward ndx start before the alias is eventually removed.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Orchestration layer correctly avoids library imports from domain packages, maintaining the four-tier hierarchy described in CLAUDE.md.
- The root contains both executable orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) and static config — these serve distinct roles but cohesion is perfect at 1.0 because none import across zones.
- Zero coupling confirms this zone is a true root with no upstream dependencies.
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm the orchestration layer is correctly isolated — all cross-package coordination happens via spawned CLIs rather than library imports.
- 29 files span config, CI, orchestration scripts, and guidelines — consider whether PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md and similar docs belong in the docs zone for cleaner separation.
- Root-level orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) delegate to package CLIs rather than importing package APIs, which is the intended architectural pattern.
- The orchestration layer's zero coupling is maintained by design — cli.js, web.js, and ci.js coordinate packages exclusively through spawned subprocesses, making the four-tier hierarchy a runtime rather than compile-time enforcement.
- Orchestration scripts in this zone are the only code path that wires together rex, sourcevision, and hench at runtime — if any cross-package CLI contract changes (flag names, exit codes, stdout format), breakage surfaces here first, not in types.
- The orchestration zone has no import edges to any other zone, meaning integration contract violations (e.g. changed CLI flags or output formats in rex/sourcevision/hench) will only manifest at runtime, not at build or typecheck time — consider adding a contract-test layer in e2e that validates CLI I/O contracts across package boundaries.
- build.js (web package) and the root orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) are all plain JavaScript in a TypeScript monorepo — type-checked API contracts from rex/sourcevision/hench cannot be enforced in these entry points, so breaking changes in package APIs silently pass typecheck until runtime.
- The gateway-enforcement scripts (check-gateway-*.mjs in scripts/) operate by regex/filesystem inspection rather than import resolution, meaning they cannot detect a gateway file that correctly re-exports but also adds logic — violating the 're-export only' rule in CLAUDE.md without tripping the enforcement check.
- Root orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) are plain JS in a TypeScript monorepo, making cross-package API breakage undetectable at typecheck time. Even a full pnpm typecheck pass will not catch broken orchestration contracts.
- config.js breaks the orchestration tier's spawn-only rule in a subtle way: unlike ci.js/web.js/cli.js which coordinate via spawned subprocesses, config.js directly implements config-merging logic (deepMerge, validators, loadJSON, saveJSON) and reads/writes .rex/config.json, .hench/config.json, .sourcevision/manifest.json, and .n-dx.json via async file I/O. It is an orchestration-tier file that encapsulates library-tier concerns, making it the only undocumented exception to the spawn-only rule.
- CODEX.md exists at root alongside CLAUDE.md and mirrors the same documentation structure but adds a Codex-specific troubleshooting section (malformed output fallback, missing usage field mapping, token mismatch diagnostics). This establishes an implicit multi-vendor AI assistant convention — one instruction file per AI vendor — that is not documented as a policy anywhere, meaning future vendor additions have no template to follow.
- web.js is documented in CLAUDE.md as a 'legacy name' alias for ndx start, but the file itself contains no @deprecated annotation, no runtime console.warn, and no redirect comment pointing to cli.js. Contributors discovering web.js without reading CLAUDE.md will treat it as a primary entry point rather than a legacy alias.
- config.js implements config-merging logic (deepMerge, loadJSON, saveJSON, validators) and directly reads/writes package config files rather than delegating to a spawned CLI, violating the orchestration tier's documented spawn-only rule. This is the only root orchestration script that breaks this invariant. Either extract a dedicated config subcommand in a domain package, or explicitly document config.js as a spawn-exempt exception in CLAUDE.md.
- web.js is a legacy alias with no deprecation marker in the file itself. Add a JSDoc @deprecated comment and a runtime console.warn to guide contributors toward ndx start before the alias is eventually removed.
- CODEX.md mirrors CLAUDE.md but with Codex-specific content — this multi-vendor instruction-file convention is established by practice but not by policy. A brief policy note in CONTRIBUTING.md describing the expected structure and naming for per-vendor AI instruction files would prevent inconsistently structured files as more vendors are supported.
- [call graph] 451 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
